advantages: Fewer columns lower training cost, inference latency, and storage in feature stores while keeping human-readable feature names for stakeholders.
Pipeline-wrapped selection enforces leakage-safe refitting in cross-validation and simplifies deployment via one serialized artifact.
Two-stage filter-then-wrapper workflows scale to wide tables by pruning junk before expensive subset search.
Embedded SelectFromModel aligns production inputs with a concrete base estimator the team already trusts (e.g. Random Forest).
disadvantages: Manual column lists maintained outside the pipeline drift from training and cause train/serve skew when schemas change.
Wrapper methods (RFE/RFECV) multiply training time and can overfit validation if too many subset sizes are tried without nested CV.
Filter pre-steps ignore interactions—features dropped early may include pairs that help the final model.
Tree importances used for selection can be unstable under correlated features and must not replace held-out metric validation.
